source: KBpedia/kbpedia v1.60 — KKO upper ontology (Charles S. Peirce universal categories)
note: The alignment that grounds Noetica's hand-rolled structures in KBpedia's formal, open Peircean upper ontology. Adopt KKO IRIs so the epistemic typing, the matter/form core, and the learning loop are standards-backed rather than ad-hoc.

property_splits_to_wikidata.note: KKO v1.50+ maps these against Wikidata properties — our typed features inherit that mapping.

epistemic_typing.comment: Noetica's induced/deduced/abduced IS Peirce's inference trichotomy (he coined 'abduction'). KKO formalizes the discovery process as kko:Methodeutic.
epistemic_typing.deduced: Peircean deduction — necessary inference (the authored textbook operators)
epistemic_typing.induced: Peircean induction — generalization from data (the symbolic-regression operators)
epistemic_typing.abduced: Peircean abduction — best-explanation hypothesis (prereq/analogy + loop hypotheses)

self_improving_loop.kko: kko:Methodeutic — 'knowledge emergence: observe & question facts → abduce hypotheses → test → new domains for inquiry'
self_improving_loop.claim: our learning loop IS Peirce's methodeutic, already formalized in OWL by KKO

entity_grounding.wikidata_linkage: versions/1.60/linkages/wikidata.n3 — ~32M entities (98% coverage) → align our 1033 glossary symbols → stable Wikidata IDs, feeding symbol-query.ts (#20)
entity_grounding.other_linkages: dbpedia / schema.org / geonames / opencyc / umbel (.n3) — additional grounding, structure-only (no Wikipedia text, per the segmentation rule)

integration_order: 1. epistemic layer  — emit KKO IRIs from type-operators.py + canon-to-ontogenesis (highest leverage)
2. entity grounding — align glossary symbols → KBpedia RC → Wikidata ID (symbol-query.ts + glossary)
3. typologies       — disjoint pos/neg feature splits for the n-furcated combiner
4. matter/form      — bind chomer/tzurah → kko:Matter/kko:Forms in the upper ontology
